chunks.text: More than fifty years later, the sights of space exploration are set even further.
NASA is advancing plans to carry out a crewed mission to Mars in the 2030 s.
Private companies, too, have entered space development in earnest.
SpaceX has developed a reusable rocket, greatly lowering the cost of launch.
But the journey to Mars is full of obstacle.
The distance from Earth to Mars, even at its closest, is about 55 million kilometers.
During the six - to - nine - month trip, astronauts are exposed to radiation
loss of gravity, and psychological isolation.
For humans to live on Mars, the supply of oxygen, water, and food is essential.
a colony is envisioned in which water is extracted from underground and crops are grown in greenhouses.
Space exploration comes at an enormous cost.
Some argue that the budget should instead be used to solve problems on Earth.
But supporters argue that space exploration creates technological innovation and brings alternative solutions to Earth's problems as well.
Space will remain the last frontier for humanity.
The challenge of going there is not only a matter of science.
It is also a philosophical question: how far can humanity go?
